background: An early publication from the Framingham Heart Study identified 51 incidences of coronary disease among 424 men with high serum cholesterol (245 mg per 100 mL and above) over a 6-year period. Among 454 men with lower cholesterol (less than 210 mg per 100 mL), there were 16 incident cases.

task.query: Calculate the proportion of coronary disease incidences among men with high serum cholesterol (245 mg per 100 mL and above).
Calculate the proportion of coronary disease incidences among men with lower serum cholesterol (less than 210 mg per 100 mL).
Calculate the relative risk (\( \widehat{R R} \)) of coronary disease for men with high serum cholesterol compared to men with lower serum cholesterol.
Provide the 95% confidence interval for the relative risk (\( \widehat{R R} \)).
task.answer: The 95% confidence interval for the \( \widehat{R R} \) is 1.98 to 5.89.
